context: Tests whether react-ban-use-effect can encode the policy into lint rules while preserving a narrow domain-specific external-integration hook with honest reactive dependencies and existing hook guardrails.

checklist.name: Import ban
checklist.description: ESLint blocks importing `useEffect` from `react` in normal application code, for example with `no-restricted-imports`

checklist.name: Namespace call ban
checklist.description: ESLint blocks `React.useEffect(...)` namespace calls in normal application code, for example with `no-restricted-syntax`

checklist.name: Hook exception scoped
checklist.description: The policy allows direct useEffect only in `src/hooks/useChatConnection.ts` or an equivalently narrow named external-integration hook path

checklist.name: Domain hook owns lifecycle
checklist.description: `useChatConnection` calls connectChat with serverUrl and roomId, opens the connection, and closes that same connection during effect cleanup

checklist.name: Reactive dependencies explicit
checklist.description: The hook accepts serverUrl and roomId explicitly and lists both in its effect dependency array so either change reconnects

checklist.name: No hidden dependency escape
checklist.description: The solution does not add a generic EffectCallback/dependency-array wrapper and does not suppress react-hooks/exhaustive-deps

checklist.name: Hooks lint preserved
checklist.description: Existing `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` and `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` enforcement is preserved or strengthened, not disabled globally

checklist.name: No unrelated bans
checklist.description: The policy does not ban unrelated APIs such as `useLayoutEffect` or non-React effect systems unless requested

checklist.name: Message points to replacements
checklist.description: Lint messages direct developers toward declarative replacements or reviewed external-integration hooks rather than dependency-array tuning

checklist.name: Verification named
checklist.description: The report says to run lint and includes positive/negative checks for blocked imports, blocked namespace calls, and the allowed domain hook
